fact: April 12 is exactly 102 days into the year - that's like counting to 102 every single morning since January 1st!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for cherry blossoms to explode into pink clouds that make entire streets look like cotton candy factories.
Why do so many baby animals show up around now? Because spring fever is real - warmer weather triggers massive animal baby-making season!
The sun rises about 6 minutes earlier than it did just one week ago in most places, like nature's alarm clock is getting more and more excited.
April 12 falls smack in the middle of Aries season, when the zodiac's ram is charging full speed ahead with fiery spring energy.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere spots, leaves are turning brilliant orange and red right now - it's like the trees are showing off before winter nap time.
Earthworms go absolutely bonkers around this date in temperate regions, squirming up from deep soil as the ground warms to perfect worm party temperature.
The moon phase on April 12 changes every year, but when it's a new moon on this date, the night sky becomes a giant black canvas perfect for stargazing.
Dandelions transform from cheerful yellow flowers into those fluffy white wish-balls right around now - nature's own magic trick happening in every yard.
In northern climates, the last sneaky frost can still zap plants on April 12, like winter's final prank before giving up completely.
Migrating butterflies like painted ladies start flooding through many regions around this time, turning highways into fluttering orange rivers.
The air pressure tends to get wonky in mid-April, creating those dramatic spring storms with lightning that looks like the sky is having a disco party.
